tex:
A quadrilateral has
(a) rotational symmetry of order 2

(b) two diagonals that are the only lines of symmetry.

Write down the geometrical name of this quadrilateral.

The diagram shows two parallel lines and a straight line.

Find the value of $x$.

A train journey starts at 2216.

The journey takes 5 hours 52 minutes.

Find the time the train journey finishes.

(a) In triangle $ABC$, $AB = 8$~cm, $AC = 7$~cm and $BC = 5$~cm.

**Using a ruler and compasses only**, construct triangle $ABC$.

The side $AB$ has been drawn for you.

(b) Measure angle $ACB$.

(c) Triangle $ABC$ is a scale drawing of a field.

(i) (d) The scale is $1:10\,000$.

Find the actual distance from $A$ to $B$.

Give your answer in kilometres.



(e) $B$ is due east of $A$.

Find the bearing of $A$ from $B$.

Expand.

$$g(3 - 2g)$$

(a) 

Write down the inequality represented in the diagram.

(b) Write down the integer values of $x$ that satisfy the inequality $-4 < 2x \leqslant 8$.

An athlete runs at a speed of 9.5~m/s.

Convert this speed into km/h.

$A$ is the point $(2, 1)$.

$$\overrightarrow{AB} = \begin{pmatrix} 2 \\ 4 \end{pmatrix}$$

Find the coordinates of $B$.

 (, )

In a sale, the prices of coats are reduced by 15\%.

(a) The original price of a coat is \$60.

Calculate the sale price of the coat.

(b) The sale price of a different coat is \$58.14.

Calculate the original price of this coat.

Some students are asked if they like football ($F$) or rugby ($R$).

The Venn diagram shows the results.

(a) Find the number of students who do not like rugby.

(b) Use set notation to describe the region containing students who like rugby but not football.

The diagram shows two vertical poles, $AE$ and $PT$, standing on horizontal ground, $AP$.

Calculate the angle of elevation of the point $T$ from the point $E$.

A cube contains a solid metal sphere.

The sphere touches all the faces of the cube.

The side length of the cube is 8~cm.

(a) Show that the volume of the sphere is $\dfrac{256}{3}\pi$~cm$^3$.

(b) Calculate the percentage of the cube that is **not** occupied by the sphere.

(c) The density of the metal of the sphere is 7.86~g/cm$^3$.



Calculate the mass of the sphere.

Give your answer in kilograms.

{[Density = mass $\div$ volume]}

$A$, $B$ and $C$ lie on a circle, centre $O$.

$TA$ and $TB$ are tangents to the circle at $A$ and $B$.

Calculate angle $ATB$.

texHtml:
The table shows some information about the heights of 50 plants.
Height ($h$~cm)$5 < h \leqslant 10$$10 < h \leqslant 12$$12 < h \leqslant 20$
Frequency32423
Calculate an estimate of the mean height.

tex:
Find the equation of the straight line that passes through the points $(2, 0)$ and $(0, 4)$.

Give your answer in the form $y = mx + c$.

The diagram shows part of the speed--time graph for an athlete in a race.

(a) Calculate the distance the athlete runs in the first 10 seconds.

(b) The length of the race is 100~m.

After 10 seconds, the athlete continues to run at a speed of 8~m/s until the end of the race.



Calculate the **total** time the athlete takes to complete the 100~m race.

The mass of a radioactive substance decays exponentially at a rate of 10\% per day.

The initial mass of the substance is 20~g.

Find the number of whole days it takes for the mass of the substance to first be less than 1~g.

These are the first five terms of a sequence.

48 \quad 24 \quad 12 \quad 6 \quad 3
(a) Find the next term.

(b) Find the $n$th term.

In triangle $STU$, $ST = 8$~cm, $SU = 9$~cm and angle $TSU = 50^\circ$.

Calculate the area of triangle $STU$.

Alex invests \$200 at a rate of $r$\% per year compound interest.

At the end of 25 years the value of this investment is \$301.10.



Find the value of $r$.

$$y \propto \frac{1}{\sqrt{x+1}}$$

When $x = 8$, $y = 3$.



Find $y$ in terms of $x$.

Martha walks a distance of 10~km at a speed of $x$~km/h.

She then runs a distance of 5~km at a speed of $(x+4)$~km/h.

The total time taken for the whole journey is 3.5 hours.

(a) Write down an expression in terms of $x$ for the time Martha is walking.

(b) Show that $7x^2 - 2x - 80 = 0$.

(c) Solve $7x^2 - 2x - 80 = 0$, giving your answers correct to 2 decimal places.

You must show all your working.

  \text{ or } 

(d) Calculate the difference between the time Martha is walking and the time she is running.

Give your answer in hours and minutes correct to the nearest minute.

Kai sorts parcels into two types, light and heavy.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Type of parcel & Mass ($m$~kg) \\
\hline
Light & $0 < m \leqslant 2$ \\
\hline
Heavy & $2 < m \leqslant 5$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{InsertScreenShot}

The histogram shows some information about the number of parcels Kai sorts in one day.

(a) Find the number of light parcels.

(b) There are 102 heavy parcels.

Complete the histogram.

Solve the simultaneous equations.

You must show all your working.

\begin{align*}
y &= 2x^2 - 3x - 7 \\
y &= 2x - 7
\end{align*}

The diagram shows a prism of length 15~cm.

The cross-section of the prism is a trapezium.

Angle $DAB = 90^\circ$ and angle $ADC = 90^\circ$.

$AB = 10$~cm, $AD = 5$~cm and $DC = 7$~cm.

Calculate the angle the diagonal $AG$ makes with the base $ABFE$.

$$f(x) = 7^{x-4}$$

Find the value of $x$ when

(a) $f(x) = 1$

(b) $f^{-1}(x) = 1$.

(a) Calculate the value of $y$.

(b) Calculate $BD$.
